title: Do Ambiguous Prompts Lead to Risky Behavior? Insights from a Home Automation Study with Individuals with Intellectual Disabilities
data:
M Andrao, D Morra, G Schiavo - 2026
Abstract Large Language Models (LLMs) can generate executable code from natural-
language descriptions, a capability that positions them as a natural enabler of End-
User Development (EUD), allowing non-programmers to define software behavior in 
natural language. However, in safety-critical domains such as home automation, the 
ability of LLMs to generate not only correct but also safe code is paramount
especially when the end users are unable to evaluate the output themselves. This

title: Effort-aware ROC Curves: a Comprehensive Approach to the Evaluation of Software Defect Prediction Models
data:
L Lavazza, S Morasca, G Rotoloni- ACM Transactions on Software Engineering and, 2026
Background: Effort-aware metrics (EAMs) have the merit of including software 
analysis effort in the evaluation of software defect prediction (SDP) models. Yet, 
almost all EAMs concentrate only on the effectiveness of SDP models, ie, how well 
they identify actually defective modules. Instead, EAMs ignore the efficiency of SDP 
models, ie, their ability to not classify non-defective modules as defective. Efficiency 
is important because modules incorrectly classified as defective will undergo useless
